# SL-PERF-005: html-size-over-guidance

<!-- Generated by scripts/generate-rule-docs.mjs. Do not edit by hand. -->

## Summary

Initial HTML size exceeds configured technical guidance.

## Metadata

- Category: Performance and technical signals (performance-technical)
- Default severity: info
- Confidence: heuristic
- Scope: page
- Provider scope: core
- Version: 1.0.0

## Evidence Sources

- http-header
- raw-html

## Required Evidence

- content-length
- raw-html-size
- policy-threshold

## When This Triggers

Measure raw HTML byte size and compare with non-blocking policy.

## False Positives

This rule is heuristic. Treat the diagnostic as a review prompt and use DSL
severity overrides or suppressions when project policy intentionally differs.

## How To Fix

Review excessive inline data, duplicated metadata, or oversized HTML payloads.

## Examples

Bad-case fixture reference:

- fixtures/performance/html-size-guidance

Fixed direction:

- Review excessive inline data, duplicated metadata, or oversized HTML payloads.

The generated documentation does not invent HTML, DSL, source locations,
selectors, or external observations beyond the catalog fixture references above.

- CLI, crawler, browser collector, overlay, and Next.js dev mode can share this
